companies.name: Anthropic
companies.summary: Day 16 of the government ban: Fable 5 still offline; Mythos 5 partially restored for Annex A (critical infrastructure) companies and agencies on June 26. Axios June 27 reported the Trump administration is close to allowing Fable 5 access 'as soon as this coming week' — Pentagon and NSA need to sign off. Anthropic accused Alibaba-affiliated operators of a 28.8 million-exchange distillation attack via ~25,000 fraudulent accounts (April 22–June 5). AI for Science event June 30 with Nobel laureate John Jumper (2 days). Biometric ID verification (Persona) July 8 (10 days). October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target; $65B Series H at $965B valuation closed May 28.

items.headline: Fable 5 still offline on Day 16; Mythos 5 cleared for Annex A critical infrastructure — Fable 5 'could lift as soon as this week'
items.detail: As of June 28, Fable 5 remains fully banned for general use (Day 16). Mythos 5 was partially restored June 26 for US organizations operating and defending critical infrastructure ('Annex A'), their foreign-national employees, Anthropic's own foreign-national staff, and US government civilian agencies and national labs. Axios reported June 27 that the Trump administration is now close to allowing Fable 5 access as soon as 'this coming week' — but the Pentagon and NSA still need to sign off. Claude Opus 4.8 remains the fallback model for general subscribers.

items.headline: Anthropic accuses Alibaba-affiliated operators of 28.8 million-exchange distillation attack via ~25,000 fraudulent accounts
items.detail: Anthropic accused operators affiliated with Alibaba of conducting a systematic model distillation attack — 28.8 million exchanges using approximately 25,000 fraudulent accounts between April 22 and June 5, 2026. The campaign was designed to extract Claude's reasoning and knowledge to train competing models. Anthropic said it detected and terminated the accounts. The accusation is one of the most detailed public disclosures of a large-scale AI model theft attempt.

items.headline: AI for Science event June 30 (2 days) with Nobel laureate John Jumper; biometric ID Persona July 8 (10 days); October IPO target
items.detail: Nobel laureate John Jumper — 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry, AlphaFold2 co-creator, joined from Google DeepMind June 19 — presents at 'The Briefing: AI for Science' virtual event June 30 at 10 AM PST (2 days away). Partners include Bristol Myers Squibb, Allen Institute for Science, and HHMI. Starting July 8 (10 days away), Anthropic can require flagged consumer Claude (Free/Pro/Max) accounts to verify identity via government-issued photo ID, live selfie, and facial geometry scan through vendor Persona — biometric pathway viewed as potential mechanism for US-verified Fable 5 restoration. October 2026 Nasdaq IPO target; confidential S-1 filed June 1. Opus 4.7 fast mode deprecated July 24.

companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.summary: Russell 3000 institutional rebalancing begins Monday June 29 (1 day away) — CoreWeave and IREN passive-fund buying starts at open. Annual Meeting proxy results (Form 8-K) due by June 30 (2 days). OpenAI-Broadcom Jalapeno chip is a new competitive threat for inference workloads. Vera Rubin in full production; NVIDIA runs 81% of TOP500 and 90% of new systems. NVIDIA holds ~$2B stake in CoreWeave (~11%) and $2.1B investment option in IREN.

items.headline: Russell 3000 institutional rebalancing begins Monday June 29 (1 day) — CoreWeave and IREN passive-fund buying starts
items.detail: The Russell 3000 reconstitution took effect after US market close on June 26. Institutional passive-fund rebalancing buying in CoreWeave (CRWV) and IREN Limited begins at Monday's open, June 29 — 1 day away. NVIDIA holds approximately $2 billion (~11%) stake in CoreWeave (purchased at $87.20/share in January 2026) and a $2.1 billion investment option in IREN. The dual inclusions trigger mandatory passive rebalancing across hundreds of index funds. CRWV pulled back to $93.50–$98.09 range after the $100.88 price at inclusion. SpaceX (SPCX) also joined the Russell 1000 at the same reconstitution.

items.headline: Annual Meeting proxy results due June 30 (2 days); OpenAI-Broadcom Jalapeno chip is new inference threat; Vera Rubin in full production
items.detail: NVIDIA held its 2026 Annual Meeting on June 24; proxy voting results (Form 8-K) are due within four business days, by June 30 — 2 days away. Seven proposals were on the ballot: director elections, PwC ratification, executive compensation, and four shareholder proposals. Separately, OpenAI's custom 'Jalapeno' AI accelerator co-developed with Broadcom in 9 months on TSMC 3nm for inference workloads represents a significant threat to NVIDIA GPU dependency for inference. Vera Rubin is in full production ramping for hyperscale AI factory deployment. NVIDIA runs 81% of the TOP500 and 90% of new systems as of June 23.

companies.name: Alphabet / Google DeepMind
companies.category: Foundation models, search, cloud, and AI research
companies.summary: Gemini 3.5 Flash released GA June 26 — first in the Gemini 3.5 series, outperforming Gemini 3.1 Pro on agentic and coding benchmarks. Gemini 3.5 Pro officially delayed to July 2026, the first publicly missed model window from Google DeepMind this year. Noam Shazeer (VP Engineering, Gemini co-lead) confirmed departed for OpenAI — the fourth senior researcher to leave in 2026. Gemini 2.5 Pro with Deep Think (82.4% GPQA Diamond) remains current flagship.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Flash released GA — outperforms Gemini 3.1 Pro on agentic, coding, and multimodal benchmarks
items.detail: Google released Gemini 3.5 Flash as the generally available model, now powering gemini-flash-latest. Gemini 3.5 Flash outperforms Gemini 3.1 Pro on Terminal-Bench 2.1 (76.2%), GDPval-AA (1656 Elo), MCP Atlas (83.6%), and leads on CharXiv (84.2%). Google describes it as 'the strongest agentic and coding model yet, excelling at complex long-horizon tasks.' Older gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview and gemini-3-pro-image-preview models were deprecated June 25. Note: this is Gemini 3.5 Flash, not 3.5 Pro.

items.headline: Gemini 3.5 Pro officially delayed to July; Noam Shazeer confirmed departed for OpenAI — fourth exit this year
items.detail: Gemini 3.5 Pro — expected to feature a 2M token context window, Deep Think reasoning, and pricing of ~$15 per million input tokens — remains officially delayed to July 2026, the first publicly missed model window from Google DeepMind this year. Noam Shazeer (VP Engineering, Gemini co-lead, 'Attention Is All You Need' co-author) confirmed his departure for OpenAI — the fourth senior researcher to leave in 2026. Gemini 2.5 Pro with Deep Think (82.4% GPQA Diamond) remains the current flagship for AI Ultra subscribers ($250/month).

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.category: Cloud AI, copilots, and enterprise software
companies.summary: Copilot Cowork Frontier grace period ends July 1 — 3 days away. Maia 200 is live at an Iowa data center (TSMC 3nm, 100B+ transistors, 10+ petaflops FP4); Anthropic negotiations ongoing — Anthropic would be first external customer. Copilot Chat now allows Claude as a model selection. Congress unveiled a bipartisan 269-page Great American Artificial Intelligence Act discussion draft proposing a three-year preemption of all state AI laws.

items.headline: Maia 200 live at Iowa data center; Anthropic would be first external customer if deal signed; MSFT +2% on news
items.detail: Microsoft has brought its Maia 200 AI accelerator online at a data center in Iowa, with a second location planned in Arizona. The chip has 100B+ transistors, delivers over 10 petaflops at 4-bit precision, built on TSMC 3nm — achieving 30% better performance-per-dollar than existing NVIDIA infrastructure for inference workloads. Negotiations with Anthropic for Maia 200 access are ongoing; if finalized, Anthropic would be Maia 200's first external customer. MSFT stock rose ~2% on the Maia 200 news.

items.headline: Copilot Cowork grace period ends July 1 (3 days); Claude in Copilot Chat; Great American AI Act preempts state laws 3 years
items.detail: Microsoft Copilot Cowork Frontier preview tenants must configure usage-based billing (Copilot Credits, $0.01/Credit) by July 1 or lose access — now 3 days away. Copilot Chat now allows users to select Anthropic's Claude as an AI model alongside OpenAI models. Congress unveiled a bipartisan 269-page Great American Artificial Intelligence Act discussion draft that would preempt all state AI laws for three years, tied to the June 2 executive order 'Promoting Advanced Artificial Intelligence Innovation and Security.'

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.summary: GPT-5.6 (Sol, Terra, Luna) launched June 26 in limited government-coordinated preview for ~20 companies — public rollout 'a couple of weeks' away per Altman. Jalapeno custom AI chip co-developed with Broadcom announced June 24, built in 9 months for inference. 2027 IPO confirmed by Bloomberg June 26. Both OpenAI and Anthropic are under the same government AI export-control framework. 42-state AG probe ongoing. ChatGPT at 900M+ weekly users but below 50% global market share for first time.

items.headline: GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna in limited government-coordinated preview — public rollout 'a couple of weeks' away
items.detail: OpenAI launched GPT-5.6 on June 26 in a limited, government-coordinated preview for approximately 20 partner companies approved by ONCD, OSTP, and Commerce (customer-by-customer approval). Three tiers: Sol (flagship, $5/$30 per 1M tokens), Terra (balanced, $2.50/$15), Luna (fast, $1/$6). Sam Altman said public release comes 'a couple of weeks later.' GPT-4.5 was retired June 27, replaced by GPT-5.3. Codex Remote — allowing users to start/continue work on a connected Mac or Windows host from ChatGPT mobile — is now GA on all plans.

items.headline: Jalapeno AI chip co-developed with Broadcom in 9 months — purpose-built for inference; Titan chip also on TSMC 3nm for Stargate
items.detail: OpenAI unveiled its first custom AI chip, 'Jalapeno,' co-developed with Broadcom in just nine months through deep software-hardware co-design. Jalapeno is a purpose-built LLM inference processor manufactured via Celestica, offering better performance-per-watt than current state-of-the-art. Mass shipments are slated for end of 2026. Broadcom is also separately fabricating OpenAI's 'Titan' chip on TSMC 3nm for H2 2026 Stargate deployment. The news contributed to a broad AI chip stock selloff on June 26–27.

items.headline: Bloomberg confirmed 2027 IPO lean; government AI dual-control framework; 42-state AG probe; ChatGPT below 50% market share
items.detail: Bloomberg confirmed June 26 that OpenAI is leaning toward a 2027 IPO; CFO Sarah Friar cited operational readiness, Stargate cash burn, and SpaceX IPO volatility. OpenAI filed a confidential S-1 June 8. The Trump administration is restricting both Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 under the same government AI export-control framework — a new era of government-supervised model releases. The 42-state AG probe (led by NY AG Letitia James) remains active. ChatGPT serves 900M+ weekly active users; market share fell to 46.4% (Sensor Tower) — first time below 50% — with Gemini at 27.7% and Claude at 10.3%.

companies.name: Meta
companies.category: Open models, consumer AI, and infrastructure
companies.summary: Meta is building an AI-powered prediction market app (codename 'Antwerp' / 'FBForecast' / 'Arena') using Llama to auto-generate questions and resolve markets — separate from Facebook and Instagram. Meta Glasses (Fury, Adventurer at $299; Starfire at $399) launched and shipping June 23. AI Mode on Facebook is live. 2026 capex guidance of $115–135B unchanged. AMD MI450 samples shipping under the $60B/6GW partnership.

items.headline: Meta building AI prediction market app 'Arena' using Llama — separate from Facebook/Instagram; UK regulators poised to scrutinize
items.detail: Meta plans to release an AI-powered prediction market app (codenamed 'Antwerp' / 'FBForecast'; product name 'Arena') where users wager on real-world events using play money. Llama will auto-generate questions from trending topics, make personalized market recommendations, and resolve markets in near-real-time. The app will be separate from Facebook and Instagram. British regulators are already poised to scrutinize it. No confirmed public launch date yet.

items.headline: Meta Glasses shipping at $299–$399; 2026 capex $115–135B; AMD MI450 samples arriving; Cannes Lions AI ad tools
items.detail: Meta Glasses (Fury, Adventurer at $299; Starfire at $399) with Muse Spark AI began shipping June 23. AI Mode on Facebook is live, pulling from public info across Meta's platforms. 2026 capex guidance of $115–135B unchanged. AMD MI450 samples are shipping under the $60B/6GW partnership with first gigawatt shipments for H2 2026. CoreWeave $21B agreement through December 2032 proceeding. At Cannes Lions 2026, Meta announced end-to-end AI creative solutions for marketers.

companies.name: Amazon / AWS
companies.summary: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy met PM Modi on June 25 and announced an additional $13B in AI and cloud investment in India, bringing the total to $48B between 2026–2030. AWS launched Bedrock Fully Managed Knowledge Bases and Web Search on AgentCore GA (US East) at AWS Summit New York. OpenAI GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4, and Codex are GA on Bedrock. Project Rainier (nearly 500,000 Trainium2 chips) remains fully operational for Claude workloads.

items.headline: Amazon India investment raised to $48B through 2030 — additional $13B announced at Modi meeting June 25
items.detail: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy met Prime Minister Narendra Modi on June 25 in New Delhi and announced an additional $13 billion in AI and cloud infrastructure investment in India, bringing the total to $48 billion committed between 2026 and 2030. The new funds expand AWS data centers in Mumbai and Hyderabad, with deployment of Trainium chips and Amazon Bedrock access for Indian AI development programs.

items.headline: AWS Summit NY: Bedrock Fully Managed Knowledge Bases GA; Web Search on AgentCore GA US East; OpenAI models GA on Bedrock
items.detail: At AWS Summit New York (late June), Amazon launched Bedrock Fully Managed Knowledge Bases with Smart Parsing and Agentic Retriever, simplifying enterprise RAG pipelines. Web Search on Bedrock AgentCore — a fully managed tool enabling agents to ground responses in current, cited web knowledge with zero data egress — is now GA in US East (N. Virginia). OpenAI GPT-5.5, GPT-5.4, and Codex are GA on Bedrock. Grok 4.3 and Claude models (outside Fable 5) remain on the multi-model roster. Bedrock AgentCore is SOC 1, 2, and 3 compliant.

companies.name: xAI / SpaceX
companies.summary: Reflection AI $150M/month Colossus 2 deal starts July 1 — 3 days away. SpaceX joined Russell 1000 at June 26 reconstitution. SPCX recovering near $153–155. The $60B Cursor acquisition is progressing toward Q3 regulatory close. Reid Hoffman stated June 28 that all 11 original xAI co-founders departed by May 2026. Colossus committed revenues from outside clients exceed $80B through 2029.

items.headline: Reflection AI $150M/month Colossus 2 deal starts July 1 (3 days); Colossus outside revenues exceed $80B through 2029
items.detail: SpaceX's $6.3 billion compute agreement with Reflection AI begins July 1 — 3 days away. Reflection pays $150 million per month for NVIDIA GB300 GPU access at Colossus 2 in Memphis through 2029, with a 90-day termination clause after the first 3 months. Reflection is working with the DoE Genesis Mission and Pentagon AI. Combined Colossus committed revenues from all outside clients (Anthropic, Google, Cursor training) now exceed $80 billion through 2029. Both Colossus 1 and Colossus 2 are fully committed to third-party customers.

items.headline: SpaceX joined Russell 1000 June 26; $60B Cursor acquisition toward Q3; all 11 original xAI co-founders departed by May
items.detail: SpaceX (SPCX) joined the Russell 1000 as part of the June 26 FTSE Russell reconstitution. SPCX is recovering near $153–155 from its $147.11 low on June 23 (ATH $225.64 on June 16 post-IPO). The $60B all-stock acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor) — announced June 16, the largest VC-backed startup acquisition ever — is progressing toward Q3 2026 regulatory close. Cursor has 50,000+ enterprise clients, ~$4B ARR, and 150M lines of daily enterprise code; Cursor is using Colossus 2 infrastructure to train its next-generation model. Reid Hoffman stated June 28 that all 11 original xAI co-founders had departed by May 2026.

companies.name: Broadcom
companies.category: AI networking, custom silicon, and infrastructure software
companies.summary: Quarterly dividend $0.65/share payable June 30 (2 days) — 15 consecutive years of increases. Q2 AI semiconductor revenue $10.8B (+143% YoY); Q3 guidance ~$16B (+200%+ YoY). OpenAI Jalapeno chip co-developed in 9 months and Titan chip fabricated on TSMC 3nm for Stargate — Broadcom is OpenAI's primary ASIC partner for both inference and large-scale compute. Apollo-Blackstone $35B AI XPV Platform operational since June 9.

items.headline: Quarterly dividend $0.65/share payable June 30 (2 days); Q2 AI revenue $10.8B +143%; Q3 guidance ~$16B
items.detail: Broadcom's $0.65 per share quarterly cash dividend — marking 15 consecutive years of increases — is payable June 30 (Monday, 2 days away) to holders of record June 22. Q2 FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue was $10.8 billion (+143% YoY); total quarterly revenue hit a record $22.19 billion (+48% YoY) with free cash flow of $10.26 billion (46% margin). Q3 AI semiconductor revenue guidance is approximately $16 billion, representing more than 200% YoY growth. FY2027 target exceeds $100B across six core custom-silicon customers.

items.headline: OpenAI Jalapeno inference chip co-developed in 9 months; Titan chip on TSMC 3nm for Stargate — Broadcom cements frontier ASIC dominance
items.detail: OpenAI's custom 'Jalapeno' AI accelerator was co-developed with Broadcom in nine months through deep software-hardware co-design, manufactured via Celestica, and designed for LLM inference workloads. Mass shipments are slated for end of 2026. Broadcom is also fabricating OpenAI's 'Titan' chip on TSMC 3nm for H2 2026 Stargate deployment. Combined, these two chips cement Broadcom as the dominant custom ASIC partner for frontier AI labs. The Apollo and Blackstone-led $35B AI XPV Platform (operational since June 9) enables 20GW+ of Broadcom XPU compute through 2028.
items.sourceTitle: CNBC / Seeking Alpha / Broadcom Investor Relations

companies.name: Palantir
companies.summary: PLTR bounced ~5.35% to $112.40 on June 26, breaking a seven-day losing streak — catalyst was Michael Burry closing half his short position. A Zeta Global partnership was announced. The Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline benefits from the Mythos 5 partial restoration; Fable 5 talks continue. PLTR remains down ~40% YTD. 2026 revenue guidance: $7.18–7.20B (+61% YoY).

items.headline: PLTR bounced 5.35% to ~$112.40 on June 26 — Michael Burry closes half his short; Zeta Global partnership announced
items.detail: Palantir (PLTR) surged approximately 5.35% on June 26, closing near $112.40 (range $107.88–$114.08), breaking a seven-day losing streak. The primary catalyst was Michael Burry disclosing he closed out half his short position. A new partnership with Zeta Global was also announced. The 52-week low was $106.38 during the selloff. PLTR remains down ~40% YTD. 2026 revenue guidance: $7.18–$7.20B (+61% YoY).

items.headline: Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline: Mythos 5 partial restoration first positive signal since June 12 ban; Fable 5 talks continue
items.detail: The Trump administration's June 26 partial restoration of Mythos 5 access for ~100 US companies and federal agencies is the first positive development for the Anthropic-Palantir-AWS defense pipeline since the June 12 ban. Palantir's Maven Smart System retains its DoD program-of-record designation. The Pentagon had reportedly migrated at least two-thirds of AI workflows off Anthropic after the ban. A broader Fable 5 restoration — expected 'as soon as this coming week' per Axios — would further benefit the pipeline. Palantir also won a U.S. Treasury Department contract June 23.

companies.name: Apple
companies.summary: iOS 27 developer beta continues expanding AI features: rebuilt Siri AI running on a custom Google Gemini model (~$1B/year), bill splitting, smart passwords, Safari tab organization, and Dynamic Island integration. OpenAI retained outside counsel over Siri ChatGPT placement — formal legal action being prepared, no lawsuit filed. Apple evaluating Mistral AI as an additional provider option. iOS 27 public beta arrives July; GA September 2026.

items.headline: iOS 27 AI features: Gemini-powered Siri, bill splitting, smart passwords, Safari tab organization — GA September 2026
items.detail: iOS 27 developer beta continues revealing practical AI features: AI-assisted bill splitting, automatic password replacement after breaches, intelligent message suggestions, calendar creation from natural language, smarter Shortcuts, contextual customer service info, grouped smart home notifications, and AI-organized Safari tabs. Rebuilt Siri AI — running on a custom ~1.2 trillion parameter Google Gemini model via a $1B/year Apple-Google deal — gets a new Dynamic Island visual experience. iOS 27 public beta arrives July; GA September 2026.

items.headline: OpenAI outside counsel retained over Siri ChatGPT placement — no lawsuit filed; Apple evaluating Mistral as additional provider
items.detail: OpenAI retained outside counsel and is preparing a potential formal breach-of-contract notice against Apple over ChatGPT placement in the new Siri AI, alleging Apple made 'no honest effort' to meet integration commitments. No lawsuit has been filed as of June 28. Apple's iOS 27 Extensions framework makes ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Mistral eligible as Siri extensions — currently disabled pending EU Digital Markets Act clarity. Apple is separately evaluating Mistral AI as an additional provider.

companies.name: AMD
companies.category: AI accelerators, CPUs, and data center silicon
companies.summary: AMD recovering at ~$551 (above Street mean ~$490) after the June 23 SK Hynix HBM4 selloff. Citi upgraded AMD to Buy with $575 target. MI450 features 432 GB HBM4, 20 TB/s bandwidth, 40 PFLOPs FP4 — Oracle Cloud will host the first public MI450 supercluster (50,000 units Q3 2026). AMD Advancing AI 2026 is July 22–23 at Moscone Center (24 days away). Q2 results August 4.

items.headline: MI450: 432 GB HBM4, 20 TB/s, 40 PFLOPs FP4 — Oracle Cloud hosting first public MI450 supercluster (50K units, Q3 2026)
items.detail: AMD's MI450 accelerator features up to 432 GB of HBM4 memory, 20 TB/s bandwidth, and 40 PFLOPs FP4 — competing directly with NVIDIA's Vera Rubin platform. Oracle Cloud Infrastructure will host the first publicly available AI supercluster running AMD Instinct MI450 GPUs, starting with 50,000 units in Q3 2026. Meta has agreed to deploy up to 6 gigawatts of AMD Instinct GPUs with MI450 samples already shipping. OpenAI's initial 1GW MI450 rollout remains on track for H2 2026.

items.headline: AMD recovering at ~$551; Citi Buy/$575; Advancing AI July 22–23 (24 days); Q2 results August 4
items.detail: AMD shares have recovered to approximately $551, above the Street mean of ~$490, after the 6.3% decline on June 23 triggered by SK Hynix slowing HBM4 expansion. Citi upgraded AMD to Buy with a $575 price target. Analysts at Goldman Sachs (Buy), Baird ($625 target), and Barclays (Street-high $665) confirmed the selloff was a supply-chain margin concern, not a demand signal — AMD's H2 2026 MI450 production timeline remains intact. AMD Advancing AI 2026 is July 22–23 at Moscone Center — 24 days away — expected to cover formal MI450 launch, EPYC Venice Zen 6 CPUs, Helios rack confirmation, and MI500 series preview. Q2 results: August 4.

companies.name: Perplexity AI
companies.summary: Comet AI browser is globally available on Mac, iOS, Android, and Windows, running on Claude Opus 4.6 by default. Comet Enterprise available via MDM. Comet Plus pays publishers 80% revenue share across visits, citations, and agent actions. Perplexity raised $200M at $20B valuation in June 2026. CEO confirms 2028 IPO; Apple acquisition talks confirmed dead. ARR $500M; 100M+ MAU.

items.headline: Comet browser global on Mac, iOS, Android, Windows — Opus 4.6 default; 80/20 publisher revenue split; $200M raised at $20B
items.detail: Perplexity's Comet AI browser is globally available on Mac, iOS, Android, and Windows, running on Claude Opus 4.6 as the default model (users may select others). Enterprise admins can deploy Comet via MDM on macOS and Windows. Comet Plus pays publishers 80% revenue across visits, citations, and agent actions — an industry-first agent-economy payout model. Recent updates include inline editing for computer-generated assets and Personal Computer on Mac (connects local files/apps to Comet). Perplexity raised $200M at $20B valuation in June 2026; total raised ~$1.72B. $750M Azure infrastructure deal in place.

companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.summary: Russell 3000 institutional rebalancing begins Monday June 29 (1 day away). CRWV pulled back to $93.50–$98.09 range after $100.88 at Russell inclusion. Backlog growing from $99.4B (Q1 2026) toward ~$131B. 2026 capex floor $31B for Vera Rubin NVL72 buildout. Nine of top 10 AI model providers are customers. Cantor Fitzgerald says CRWV is set to beat Q2 consensus backlog estimates.

items.headline: Russell 3000 rebalancing begins Monday June 29 (1 day); CRWV near $98 after $100.88 at inclusion; SpaceX joined Russell 1000
items.detail: CoreWeave (CRWV) entered the Russell 3000 effective after market close June 26. Institutional passive-fund rebalancing buying begins at Monday's open, June 29 — 1 day away. CRWV pulled back to the $93.50–$98.09 trading range after the $100.88 price at inclusion, with average analyst 12-month target of $138.56. SpaceX (SPCX) also joined the Russell 1000 at the same June 26 reconstitution. IREN Limited also joined the Russell 3000.

items.headline: Backlog growing toward ~$131B from $99.4B in Q1; capex floor $31B; Cantor Fitzgerald bullish on Q2 backlog beat; 9 of top 10 AI providers
items.detail: CoreWeave's contracted backlog was $99.4 billion as of Q1 2026, growing toward approximately $131 billion as new Meta, Anthropic, and OpenAI Vera Rubin NVL72 contracts are added. Cantor Fitzgerald analyst Brett Knoblauch says CRWV is set to beat Q2 consensus backlog estimates. The 2026 capex floor is $31 billion. A $3.1 billion HPC loan was recently secured. Nine of the top ten AI model providers are CoreWeave customers. Nasdaq-100 inclusion took effect June 22 (closed at $117.03, +9.67%).

companies.name: Mistral AI
companies.summary: Paris Bruyères-le-Châtel data center (13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPUs, 44 MW, ~276 exaflops FP4): June 28 is the last day of the end-of-June opening window — no official opening announcement found. NVIDIA-Mistral Compute sovereign AI cloud launched VivaTech June 20 (18,000 Grace Blackwell systems). $830M debt facility. CEO Mensch leveraging Fable/Mythos ban for European AI sovereignty narrative. Mistral targets EUR 1B in 2026 revenue.

items.headline: Paris data center: June 28 is last day of end-of-June opening window — no official announcement found as of today
items.detail: Mistral AI's Bruyères-le-Châtel data center near Paris (44 MW, 13,800 NVIDIA GB300 GPU systems, operated by Eclairion, ~276 exaflops FP4) was targeted to open 'by end of June 2026.' As of June 28 — the last day of the window — no official opening announcement has been made. The facility is the first node in Mistral's 200 MW European compute roadmap (extending to 1.4 GW before 2030). A secondary Les Ulis 10 MW inference facility is scheduled for Q3 2026. The facility may have opened quietly or an announcement is imminent.

items.headline: NVIDIA-Mistral Compute sovereign cloud live since VivaTech June 20; $830M debt facility; iOS 27 Extensions makes Mistral Siri-eligible
items.detail: NVIDIA and Mistral AI launched Mistral Compute — a sovereign AI GPU cloud platform for European governments and enterprises — at VivaTech 2026 (June 20), built on 18,000 NVIDIA Grace Blackwell systems. Mistral is a founding member of the NVIDIA Nemotron Coalition. The $830M debt facility (led by Bpifrance, including BNP Paribas, Credit Agricole CIB, HSBC, La Banque Postale, MUFG, Natixis) is the largest AI-focused borrowing by a European tech company. Apple's iOS 27 Extensions framework lists Mistral as an eligible Siri extension — currently disabled pending EU DMA clarity. CEO Mensch: 'We exist to make sure that everyone gets access to the best AI systems, outside of centralised control.'

companies.name: Tesla
companies.category: Autonomous vehicles, robotics, and applied AI
companies.summary: Cybercab at 102+ units covering Austin (full metro), Dallas, Houston, and SF Bay Area. Service approaching one-year anniversary with mixed reviews: long waits, occasional incorrect pickup/dropoff. Optimus Gen 3 in mass production at Fremont; V3 full-body production targets summer 2026. Terafab in-house AI5 chip fab announced March 21. TSLA trading near flat at ~$375.

items.headline: Cybercab 102+ units; full Austin metro, Dallas, Houston, SF Bay; mixed reviews approaching one-year anniversary
items.detail: Tesla's Cybercab service has 102+ units covering the full Austin metro, Dallas, Houston, and SF Bay Area. Approaching its one-year anniversary, early results are mixed — Insurance Journal (June 11) reported long wait times, limited availability, and occasional incorrect pickup or dropoff locations. Elon Musk told investors June 20 to be 'really patient' with the Cybercab ramp. EPA specs: 47.6 kWh battery, 163 kW motor, 418-mile unadjusted range (293-mile adjusted).

items.headline: Optimus Gen 3 mass production at Fremont; V3 full-body targets summer 2026; Terafab AI5 chip fab; first external B2B customers late 2026
items.detail: Tesla Optimus Gen 3 entered mass production at Fremont January 21, 2026. Gen 3 hands are in active 24/7 factory deployment. V3 full-body production is targeted for summer 2026. First external commercial (enterprise/B2B) deployments are targeted for late 2026. Giga Texas North Campus expansion (5.2M sq ft) targets 10M robots/year. Tesla's Terafab initiative (announced March 21) is an in-house semiconductor fab for the AI5 chip powering Optimus Gen 3.

ipoTracker.status: Day 16 of Fable ban. Mythos 5 cleared for Annex A (critical infrastructure) June 26. Fable 5 'could lift as soon as this coming week' per Axios June 27. AI for Science June 30 (2 days). Biometric ID July 8 (10 days). October 2026 Nasdaq IPO; $65B Series H at $965B valuation.

partnershipTracker.type: Structured finance platform for Broadcom AI XPU deployment
partnershipTracker.status: Operational — $35B capital solution; enables 20GW+ Broadcom XPU compute for Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028. Broadcom dividend payable June 30 (2 days). Broadcom co-developing OpenAI Jalapeno chip and fabricating Titan chip.
partnershipTracker.terms: Apollo Global Management and Blackstone lead a $35 billion capital solution for the Broadcom AI XPV Platform, enabling more than 20 gigawatts of global AI compute capacity using Broadcom XPUs for hyperscalers including Anthropic and OpenAI through 2028. Platform operational since June 9. Anthropic's 1+ gigawatt compute expansion at Fluidstack-based sites beginning mid-2026 is among the first deployments. Broadcom is also co-developing the OpenAI 'Jalapeno' custom AI accelerator (built in 9 months, TSMC 3nm) and fabricating OpenAI's 'Titan' chip for Stargate deployment.
partnershipTracker.confidence: Confirmed by Apollo press release and Broadcom announcement
partnershipTracker.note: Broadcom $0.65 quarterly dividend payable June 30 (2 days). Jalapeno and Titan chip co-development cements Broadcom's position as the leading custom ASIC partner for frontier AI labs.

latestArticle.title: Broadcom, Apollo, and Blackstone Establish Landmark Strategic Platform to Accelerate 20GW+ of Global AI Deployments
